tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows significantly elevated creatinine, proteinuria, and microalbuminuria, suggesting impaired renal function. Low total CO₂ and high chloride may indicate metabolic acidosis. Urinalysis reveals cloudy, brown urine with protein, glucose, ketones, and bilirubin, but no infection markers. Lipid profile shows hypercholesterolemia and high LDL. HbA1c is unexpectedly low despite glycosuria. Findings are consistent with possible diabetic nephropathy and chronic kidney disease; clinical correlation and further nephrology evaluation are recommended.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Nonproliferative retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
